title: Testing, Fault Injection, Release, and Production Defense · Language Lens: Pure Contract Property Tests
summary: A service passes mocked unit tests and a local emulator demo, then deploys new SDK and CDN settings without race, AWS contract, security, load, rollback, restore, or cost evidence. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pure contract property tests increment in production file-delivery defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Use table, property, and fuzz tests for key, range, policy, lifecycle, state-machine, checksum, retry, and cache invariants with changed cases.
Forecast request, byte, concurrency, part, KMS, origin, egress, log, and budget limits and connect alerts to admission, degradation, and owner actions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Pure Contract Property Tests · Theory Pure Contract Property Tests
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5461 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on deterministic, Go runtime, controlled AWS, fault, load, restore, and rollback evidence; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Pure Contract Property Tests contract for the supplied data.
